title: elymus kengii
intro: Elymus kengii, also known as Keng's wildrye, is a species of wild grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is a perennial plant native to China, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Elymus kengii has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which are believed to promote overall health and prevent various diseases. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system.
